devanagari: राजसूये भगवतोऽग्रपूजनं ततो रुष्टस्य दुर्वदत्तः शिशुपालस्य भगवता वधश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं युधिष्ठिरो राजा जरासन्धवधं विभोः । कृष्णस्य ...
english_devnagari: rājasūye bhagavato śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ yudhiṣṭhiro rājā jarāsandhavadhaṃ vibhoḥ kṛṣṇasya cānubhāvaṃ taṃ śrutvā prītastamabravīt

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus heard about the slaying of Jarāsandha and also about Lord Kṛṣṇa’s supreme glory, king Yudhiṣṭhira was highly delighted. He addressed him as follows.

devanagari: श्रीयुधिष्ठिर उवाच । ये स्युस्त्रैलोक्यगुरवः सर्वे लोकमहेश्वराः । वहन्ति दुर्लभं लब्ध्वा शिरसैवानुशासनम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīyudhiṣṭhira uvāca ye syustrailokyaguravaḥ sarve lokamaheśvarāḥ vahanti durlabhaṃ labdhvā śirasaivānuśāsanam

translation:
Yudhiṣṭhira said:
All the preceptors of the three worlds (like Sanaka and others) and all the great gods, the protectors of different worlds, do bear on their heads your commands when they have the privilege to receive that Grace which is rarely obtained by others.

devanagari: स भवान् अरविन्दाक्षो दीनानां ईशमानिनाम् । धत्तेऽनुशासनं भूमन् तदत्यन्तविडम्बनम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bhavān aravindākṣo dīnānāṃ īśamāninām dhatte

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: O Lord of infinite prowess and glory! That lotuseyed personage like you bears and carries out commands of factually indigent persons who regard themselves conceitedly as rulers, is itself an extreme mockery unbecoming of you as below dignity.

devanagari: न ह्येकस्याद्वितीयस्य ब्रह्मणः परमात्मनः । कर्मभिर्वर्धते तेजो ह्रसते च यथा रवेः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyekasyādvitīyasya brahmaṇaḥ paramātmanaḥ karmabhirvardhate tejo hrasate ca yathā raveḥ
translation: But you are the Supreme Brahman like unto the effulgence of the Sun, the glory of the Brahman, the Supreme Self who is one, without a second is neither enhanced nor -diminished by the performance of acts by you.

devanagari: न वै तेऽजित भक्तानां ममाहमिति माधव । त्वं तवेति च नानाधीः पशूनामिव वैकृती ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: na vai te tvaṃ taveti ca nānādhīḥ paśūnāmiva vaikṛtī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: O unconquerable Lord of Lakṣmī! Just as the beasts (or ignorant people) entertain the (perverse) notion of difference pertaining to their body indexed by such terms as “I and mine”, “You and yours”, even your devotees do not possess such a crooked notion of difference (Need it be said that it is totally absent in you?)

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युक्त्वा यज्ञिये काले वव्रे युक्तान् स ऋत्विजः । कृष्णानुमोदितः पार्थो ब्राह्मणान् ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityuktvā yajñiye kāle vavre yuktān sa ṛtvijaḥ kṛṣṇānumoditaḥ pārtho brāhmaṇān brahmavādinaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Addressing Kṛṣṇa thus and with his approval, Kuntī’s son, Yudhiṣṭhira invited competent and worthy Brāhmaṇas who were proficient in the Vedas and the sacrificial ritual, to officiate as priests (at the Rājasūya sacrifice to be performed) in a period suitable for the celebration of such sacrifices such as the spring.

devanagari: द्वैपायनो भरद्वाजः सुमन्तुर्गोतमोऽसितः । वसिष्ठश्च्यवनः कण्वो मैत्रेयः कवषस्त्रितः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dvaipāyano bharadvājaḥ sumanturgotamo vasiṣṭhaścyavanaḥ kaṇvo maitreyaḥ kavaṣastritaḥ
translation: ( Kṛṣṇa-) Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), Bharadvāja, Sumantu, Gautama, Asita, Vasiṣṭha, Cyavana, Kaṇva, Maitreya, Kavaṣa, Trita, Viśvāmitra, Vāmadeva, Sumati, Jaimini, Kratu, Paila. Parāśara, Garga, Vaiśampāyana, Atharvan, Kāśyapa, Dhaumya, Paraśurāma (of the Bhṛgu clan), Āsuri, Vītihotra, Madhucchandas, Vīrasena, Akṛtavraṇa—these and many others were invited.

devanagari: विश्वामित्रो वामदेवः सुमतिर्जैमिनिः क्रतुः । पैलः पराशरो गर्गो वैशम्पायन एव च ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: viśvāmitro vāmadevaḥ sumatirjaiminiḥ kratuḥ pailaḥ parāśaro gargo vaiśampāyana eva ca
translation: ( Kṛṣṇa-) Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), Bharadvāja, Sumantu, Gautama, Asita, Vasiṣṭha, Cyavana, Kaṇva, Maitreya, Kavaṣa, Trita, Viśvāmitra, Vāmadeva, Sumati, Jaimini, Kratu, Paila. Parāśara, Garga, Vaiśampāyana, Atharvan, Kāśyapa, Dhaumya, Paraśurāma (of the Bhṛgu clan), Āsuri, Vītihotra, Madhucchandas, Vīrasena, Akṛtavraṇa—these and many others were invited.

devanagari: अथर्वा कश्यपो धौम्यो रामो भार्गव आसुरिः । वीतिहोत्रो मधुच्छन्दा वीरसेनोऽकृतव्रणः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: atharvā kaśyapo dhaumyo rāmo bhārgava āsuriḥ vītihotro madhucchandā vīraseno
translation: ( Kṛṣṇa-) Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), Bharadvāja, Sumantu, Gautama, Asita, Vasiṣṭha, Cyavana, Kaṇva, Maitreya, Kavaṣa, Trita, Viśvāmitra, Vāmadeva, Sumati, Jaimini, Kratu, Paila. Parāśara, Garga, Vaiśampāyana, Atharvan, Kāśyapa, Dhaumya, Paraśurāma (of the Bhṛgu clan), Āsuri, Vītihotra, Madhucchandas, Vīrasena, Akṛtavraṇa—these and many others were invited.

devanagari: उपहूतास्तथा चान्ये द्रोणभीष्मकृपादयः । धृतराष्ट्रः सहसुतो विदुरश्च महामतिः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: upahūtāstathā cānye droṇabhīṣmakṛpādayaḥ dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ sahasuto viduraśca mahāmatiḥ
translation: (And also were invited) Droṇa, Bhīṣma, Kṛpa and others, Dhṛtarāṣṭra with all his sons and the noble-minded Vidura.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणाः क्षत्रिया वैश्याः शूद्रा यज्ञदिदृक्षवः । तत्रेयुः सर्वराजानो राज्ञां प्रकृतयो नृप ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇāḥ kṣatriyā vaiśyāḥ śūdrā yajñadidṛkṣavaḥ tatreyuḥ sarvarājāno rājñāṃ prakṛtayo nṛpa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[optative active third plural], [perfect active third plural]

translation: As the tradition goes, all the utensils used in this sacrifice were of gold as they were at Varuṇa’s Rājasūya sacrifice in ancient times. Indra and other protecting deities of different spheres, god Brahmā and Śiva along with their attendants, Siddhas and Gandharvas (celestial singers), Vidyādharas and big serpents, sages, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, birds, Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, kings along with their queens—all of them were invited and they attended the Rājasūya sacrifice of Pāṇḍu’s son, Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: ततस्ते देवयजनं ब्राह्मणाः स्वर्णलाङ्गलैः । कृष्ट्वा तत्र यथाम्नायं दीक्षयां चक्रिरे नृपम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tataste devayajanaṃ brāhmaṇāḥ svarṇalāṅgalaiḥ kṛṣṭvā tatra yathāmnāyaṃ dīkṣayāṃ cakrire nṛpam
translation: As the tradition goes, all the utensils used in this sacrifice were of gold as they were at Varuṇa’s Rājasūya sacrifice in ancient times. Indra and other protecting deities of different spheres, god Brahmā and Śiva along with their attendants, Siddhas and Gandharvas (celestial singers), Vidyādharas and big serpents, sages, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, birds, Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, kings along with their queens—all of them were invited and they attended the Rājasūya sacrifice of Pāṇḍu’s son, Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: हैमाः किलोपकरणा वरुणस्य यथा पुरा । इन्द्रादयो लोकपाला विरिञ्चिभवसंयुताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: haimāḥ kilopakaraṇā varuṇasya yathā purā indrādayo lokapālā viriñcibhavasaṃyutāḥ
translation: As the tradition goes, all the utensils used in this sacrifice were of gold as they were at Varuṇa’s Rājasūya sacrifice in ancient times. Indra and other protecting deities of different spheres, god Brahmā and Śiva along with their attendants, Siddhas and Gandharvas (celestial singers), Vidyādharas and big serpents, sages, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, birds, Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, kings along with their queens—all of them were invited and they attended the Rājasūya sacrifice of Pāṇḍu’s son, Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: सगणाः सिद्धगन्धर्वा विद्याधरमहोरगाः । मुनयो यक्षरक्षांसि खगकिन्नरचारणाः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sagaṇāḥ siddhagandharvā vidyādharamahoragāḥ munayo yakṣarakṣāṃsi khagakinnaracāraṇāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

translation: As the tradition goes, all the utensils used in this sacrifice were of gold as they were at Varuṇa’s Rājasūya sacrifice in ancient times. Indra and other protecting deities of different spheres, god Brahmā and Śiva along with their attendants, Siddhas and Gandharvas (celestial singers), Vidyādharas and big serpents, sages, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, birds, Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, kings along with their queens—all of them were invited and they attended the Rājasūya sacrifice of Pāṇḍu’s son, Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: राजानश्च समाहूता राजपत्‍न्यश्च सर्वशः । राजसूयं समीयुः स्म राज्ञः पाण्डुसुतस्य वै ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: rājānaśca samāhūtā rājapat‍nyaśca sarvaśaḥ rājasūyaṃ samīyuḥ sma rājñaḥ pāṇḍusutasya vai

translation: As the tradition goes, all the utensils used in this sacrifice were of gold as they were at Varuṇa’s Rājasūya sacrifice in ancient times. Indra and other protecting deities of different spheres, god Brahmā and Śiva along with their attendants, Siddhas and Gandharvas (celestial singers), Vidyādharas and big serpents, sages, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, birds, Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, kings along with their queens—all of them were invited and they attended the Rājasūya sacrifice of Pāṇḍu’s son, Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: मेनिरे कृष्णभक्तस्य सूपपन्नमविस्मिताः । अयाजयन् महाराजं याजका देववर्चसः । राजसूयेन विधिवत् प्रचेतसमिवामराः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: menire kṛṣṇabhaktasya sūpapannamavismitāḥ ayājayan mahārājaṃ yājakā devavarcasaḥ rājasūyena vidhivat pracetasamivāmarāḥ

translation: They were not surprised at all, as they thought that such a performance is quite natural to the devotees of Kṛṣṇa (to whom nothing is impossible). The sacrificial priests who looked brilliant like gods, conducted for the king, the Rājasūya sacrifice according to śāstric injunctions, as the gods did for the sake of Varuṇa.

devanagari: सूत्येऽहन्यवनीपालो याजकान् सदसस्पतीन् । अपूजयन् महाभागान् यथावत् सुसमाहितः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sūtye apūjayan mahābhāgān yathāvat susamāhitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]

translation: On the day fixed for extraction of the Soma juice, the king with serene mind duly worshipped the highly respectable Brāhmaṇas conducting the sacrifice as well as the judges of the sacrificial performance.

devanagari: सदस्याग्र्यार्हणार्हं वै विमृशन्तः सभासदः । नाध्यगच्छन् नन्ननैकान्त्यात् सहदेवस्तदाब्रवीत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sadasyāgryārhaṇārhaṃ vai vimṛśantaḥ sabhāsadaḥ nādhyagacchan nannanaikāntyāt sahadevastadābravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: The members of the assembly could not arrive at a unanimous decision as to who should be regarded as worthy of receiving the first honour of worship as there were many deserving claimants. At this juncture, Sahadeva spoke out.

devanagari: अर्हति ह्यच्युतः श्रैष्ठ्यं भगवान्सात्वतां पतिः । एष वै देवताः सर्वा देशकालधनादयः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: arhati hyacyutaḥ śraiṣṭhyaṃ bhagavānsātvatāṃ patiḥ eṣa vai devatāḥ sarvā deśakāladhanādayaḥ

translation: “(It is my considered opinion that) the imperishable Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Lord and protector of Sātvatas deserves this highest honour. For he is not only all deities but also Time, space, wealth and other things.

devanagari: यस् आत्मकमिदं विश्वं क्रतवश्च यदात्मकाः । अग्निराहुतयो मंत्राः साङ्ख्यं योगश्च यत्परः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yas ātmakamidaṃ viśvaṃ kratavaśca yadātmakāḥ agnirāhutayo maṃtrāḥ sāṅkhyaṃ yogaśca yatparaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: This universe is his manifestation. He constitutes the sacrifices. The sacrificial fire, oblations offered in the fire, the mantras recited at that time and the paths of knowledge and Yoga or devotion are meant for him.

devanagari: एक एवाद्वितीयोऽसौ अवैतदात्म्यमिदं जगत् । आत्मनात्माश्रयः सभ्याः सृजत्यवति हन्त्यजः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: eka evādvitīyo ātmanātmāśrayaḥ sabhyāḥ sṛjatyavati hantyajaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: He is One without a second; the universe is his manifestation O members of the assembly. Entirely dependent on himself and though himself unborn, he creates, protects and dissolves this universe.

devanagari: विविधानीह कर्माणि जनयन् यदवेक्षया । ईहते यदयं सर्वः श्रेयो धर्मादिलक्षणम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vividhānīha karmāṇi janayan yadavekṣayā īhate yadayaṃ sarvaḥ śreyo dharmādilakṣaṇam

translation: It is through his Grace that people perform different actions, penances and practices and everyone tries to achieve their high objects in life such as dharma (religious practices), artha (wealth), kāma (worldly enjoyment) and mokṣa (Final emancipation).

devanagari: तस्मात् कृष्णाय महते दीयतां परमार्हणम् । एवं चेत्सर्वभूतानां आत्मनश्चार्हणं भवेत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt kṛṣṇāya mahate dīyatāṃ paramārhaṇam evaṃ cetsarvabhūtānāṃ ātmanaścārhaṇaṃ bhavet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: Therefore, this highest honour ought to be offered to Lord Kṛṣṇa who is the greatest of the great. It is thus by offering him the honour of the first worship in the assembly that we shall be honouring all created beings as well as ourselves.

devanagari: सर्वभूतात्मभूताय कृष्णायानन्यदर्शिने । देयं शान्ताय पूर्णाय दत्तस्यानन्त्यमिच्छता ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvabhūtātmabhūtāya kṛṣṇāyānanyadarśine deyaṃ śāntāya pūrṇāya dattasyānantyamicchatā

translation: He who desires to reap infinite merit in return for his gift should offer it to Lord Kṛṣṇa who constitutes the Soul of all created beings and looks upon all as his own self, (or attached to his devotees) quite serene (desireless) and absolutely perfect.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा सहदेवोऽभूत् तूष्णीं कृष्णानुभाववित् । तच्छ्रुत्वा तुष्टुवुः सर्वे साधु साध्विति सत्तमाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā sahadevo tacchrutvā tuṣṭuvuḥ sarve sādhu sādhviti sattamāḥ
translation: Sahadeva, who knew the supreme greatness of Kṛṣṇa addressed them in this way and observed silence. Hearing that all excellent and pious members of the assembly were satisfied and approved of the speech with the words “well said, well expressed”,

devanagari: श्रुत्वा द्विजेरितं राजा ज्ञात्वा हार्दं सभासदाम् । समर्हयद्धृषीकेशं प्रीतः प्रणयविह्वलः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā dvijeritaṃ rājā jñātvā hārdaṃ sabhāsadām samarhayaddhṛṣīkeśaṃ prītaḥ praṇayavihvalaḥ

translation: Hearing the decision of the Brāhmaṇas and ascertaining the consensus (lit. what was desired in the hearts) of the members of the sacrificial assembly, king Yudhiṣṭhira was highly pleased. Overwhelmed with affection, he worshipped Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of senses (as the foremost member of that assembly).

devanagari: तत्पादाववनिज्यापः शिरसा लोकपावनीः । सभार्यः सानुजामात्यः सकुटुम्बो वहन्मुदा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatpādāvavanijyāpaḥ śirasā lokapāvanīḥ sabhāryaḥ sānujāmātyaḥ sakuṭumbo vahanmudā

translation: Then having washed the feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) along with his queens, younger brothers, counsellors and members of his family, delightfully bore on their heads (drops of) that water (with which he washed the Lord’s feet) which was capable of purifying the world.

devanagari: वासोभिः पीतकौषेयैः भूषणैश्च महाधनैः । अर्हयित्वाश्रुपूर्णाक्षो नाशकत् समवेक्षितुम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsobhiḥ pītakauṣeyaiḥ bhūṣaṇaiśca mahādhanaiḥ arhayitvāśrupūrṇākṣo nāśakat samavekṣitum
translation: He worshipped him by offering yellow silken cloths, and very valuable ornaments. But he could not see him clearly as his eyes were flowing with tears (of affection and joy).

devanagari: इत्थं सभाजितं वीक्ष्य सर्वे प्राञ्जलयो जनाः । नमो जयेति नेमुस्तं निपेतुः पुष्पवृष्टयः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ sabhājitaṃ vīkṣya sarve prāñjalayo janāḥ namo jayeti nemustaṃ nipetuḥ puṣpavṛṣṭayaḥ

translation: Seeing Kṛṣṇa so worshipped, all people (in the assembly) folded their palms and offered him salutations with the words Namaḥ (Bow to you), Jaya (Victory to you). And showers of flowers fell down (from the sky).

devanagari: इत्थं निशम्य दमघोषसुतः स्वपीठाद् । उत्थाय कृष्णगुणवर्णनजातमन्युः । उत्क्षिप्य बाहुमिदमाह सदस्यमर्षी । संश्रावयन् भगवते परुषाण्यभीतः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ niśamya damaghoṣasutaḥ svapīṭhād utthāya kṛṣṇaguṇavarṇanajātamanyuḥ utkṣipya bāhumidamāha sadasyamarṣī saṃśrāvayan bhagavate paruṣāṇyabhītaḥ

translation: Hearing this (above-mentioned) shouts of salutation and victory, Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, whose anger was provoked by the glorification of Kṛṣṇa’s excellences, rose from his seat. He raised up his arm. Being enraged he fearlessly uttered harsh words (with reference to and) for the hearing of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: ईशो दुरत्ययः काल इति सत्यवती श्रुतिः । वृद्धानामपि यद् बुद्धिः बालवाक्यैर्विभिद्यते ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: īśo duratyayaḥ kāla iti satyavatī śrutiḥ vṛddhānāmapi yad buddhiḥ bālavākyairvibhidyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: “The Vedic text that the Time-spirit is the real controller (capable of bringing about undesirable events) and that it is inviolable, is quite true. (For example, here, in this assembly) even the judgment of the elderly people has become vitiated by the prattling of an ignorant junior (like Sahadeva).

devanagari: यूयं पात्रविदां श्रेष्ठा मा मन्ध्वं बालभाषीतम् । सदसस्पतयः सर्वे कृष्णो यत् सम्मतोऽर्हणे ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yūyaṃ pātravidāṃ śreṣṭhā mā mandhvaṃ bālabhāṣītam sadasaspatayaḥ sarve kṛṣṇo yat sammato

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O leaders of the assembly! You are the best judges of deciding who should be the proper recepient (of this highest honour). Please do not head the childish talk of that ignorant boy Sahadeva believing in which you consented that Kṛṣṇa deserved this honour.

devanagari: तपोविद्याव्रतधरान् ज्ञानविध्वस्तकल्मषान् । परमऋषीन् ब्रह्मनिष्ठान् लोकपालैश्च पूजितान् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tapovidyāvratadharān jñānavidhvastakalmaṣān paramaṛṣīn brahmaniṣṭhān lokapālaiśca pūjitān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: [1] To the utter disregard of the leaders of this assembly who are endowed with austers penance, learning, observance of sacred vows,—members who have cleared all their sins by their spiritual knowledge; the great sages who are established in Brahman and who are worshipped even by the guardian deities of different spheres, how is a cowherd, a disgrace to his clan and family, considered eligible to receive this honour? How can a crow deserve to receive Puroḍāśa (a sacrificial oblation)?
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā takes pains to show that the inner meaning of these verses conveys the glorification of Lord Kṛṣṇa as Śiśupāla was his attendant in his original birth and as such he would naturally praise the Lord. It appears that members of the assembly shut their ears, thinking it to be defamation of the Lord; the clans of Matsya, Kekaya, Sṛñjayas did not understand this double meaning and wanted to attack Śiśupāla. And Lord Kṛṣṇa also did not understand his praise and beheaded his servant who was praising him in words of double meaning and marched against him with a sword (vide verses 38-43 below). In supra 3.18-3-8 where Hiraṇyākṣa upbraided Lord Viṣṇu in the boar form, Bhāvāratha Dīpikā has tried to read Viṣṇu’s praise in it, vide v.l. I pp. 320-321.

devanagari: सदस्पतीन् अतिक्रम्य गोपालः कुलपांसनः । यथा काकः पुरोडाशं सपर्यां कथमर्हति ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sadaspatīn atikramya gopālaḥ kulapāṃsanaḥ yathā kākaḥ puroḍāśaṃ saparyāṃ kathamarhati
translation: [1] To the utter disregard of the leaders of this assembly who are endowed with austers penance, learning, observance of sacred vows,—members who have cleared all their sins by their spiritual knowledge; the great sages who are established in Brahman and who are worshipped even by the guardian deities of different spheres, how is a cowherd, a disgrace to his clan and family, considered eligible to receive this honour? How can a crow deserve to receive Puroḍāśa (a sacrificial oblation)?
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā takes pains to show that the inner meaning of these verses conveys the glorification of Lord Kṛṣṇa as Śiśupāla was his attendant in his original birth and as such he would naturally praise the Lord. It appears that members of the assembly shut their ears, thinking it to be defamation of the Lord; the clans of Matsya, Kekaya, Sṛñjayas did not understand this double meaning and wanted to attack Śiśupāla. And Lord Kṛṣṇa also did not understand his praise and beheaded his servant who was praising him in words of double meaning and marched against him with a sword (vide verses 38-43 below). In supra 3.18-3-8 where Hiraṇyākṣa upbraided Lord Viṣṇu in the boar form, Bhāvāratha Dīpikā has tried to read Viṣṇu’s praise in it, vide v.l. I pp. 320-321.

devanagari: वर्णाश्रमकुलापेतः सर्वधर्मबहिष्कृतः । स्वैरवर्ती गुणैर्हीनः सपर्यां कथमर्हति ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: varṇāśramakulāpetaḥ sarvadharmabahiṣkṛtaḥ svairavartī guṇairhīnaḥ saparyāṃ kathamarhati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hā class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ hā class 3 verb]

translation: He does not belong to any class of society, (varṇa), stage of life (āśrama) or nobility of birth. He has been excommunicated by (i.e. beyond the pale of) all dharmas. He behaves wantonly (against moral or religious code of conduct) and is devoid of virtues. How does he deserve worship?

devanagari: ययातिनैषां हि कुलं शप्तं सद्भिर्बहिष्कृतम् । वृथापानरतं शश्वत् सपर्यां कथमर्हति ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yayātinaiṣāṃ hi kulaṃ śaptaṃ sadbhirbahiṣkṛtam vṛthāpānarataṃ śaśvat saparyāṃ kathamarhati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ śap class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śap class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ śap class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śap class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śap class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śap class 4 verb]

translation: His family has been cursed by Yayāti (the founder of his clan) and is excommunicated by all good people. He is always indulged in drinking. How is he eligible for this worship?

devanagari: ब्रह्मर्षिसेवितान् देशान् हित्वैतेऽब्रह्मवर्चसम् । समुद्रं दुर्गमाश्रित्य बाधन्ते दस्यवः प्रजाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmarṣisevitān deśān hitvaite samudraṃ durgamāśritya bādhante dasyavaḥ prajāḥ
translation: These marauders have given up their country (Śūrasena i.e. region around Mathurā)—a land frequented and inhabited by Brāhmaṇa sages; they have resorted to their citadel in the sea—a place destitute of Brāhmaṇas devoted to Vedic studies and harass all people from that strong-hold.”

devanagari: एवं आदीन्यभद्राणि बभाषे नष्टमङ्गलः । नोवाच किञ्चिद् भगवान् यथा सिंहः शिवारुतम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ ādīnyabhadrāṇi babhāṣe naṣṭamaṅgalaḥ novāca kiñcid bhagavān yathā siṃhaḥ śivārutam

translation: Śiśupāla whose fund of auspicious acts and merits was exhausted, spoke these and the like defamatory words to Kṛṣṇa. But the Lord did not respond at all as a lion does to the howling of female jackals.

devanagari: भगवन् निन्दनं श्रुत्वा दुःसहं तत्सभासदः । कर्णौ पिधाय निर्जग्मुः शपन्तश्चेदिपं रुषा ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavan nindanaṃ śrutvā duḥsahaṃ tatsabhāsadaḥ karṇau pidhāya nirjagmuḥ śapantaścedipaṃ ruṣā

translation: Hearing that unbearable censuring of the Lord, the members of the assembly closed their ears and left the assembly, censuring Śiśupāla angrily.

devanagari: निन्दां भगवतः श्रृण्वन् तत्परस्य जनस्य वा । ततो नापैति यः सोऽपि यात्यधः सुकृताच्च्युतः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: nindāṃ bhagavataḥ śrṛṇvan tatparasya janasya vā tato nāpaiti yaḥ so

translation: When matters came to such a stage the Lord himself stood up in the meanwhile and restraining his own. partisans, angrily cut off the head of his advancing enemy with his sharp-edged discus—Sudarśana.

translation: There was a great uproar when Śiśupāla was killed. In that bustle, the kings who were his followers ran away to save their lives.

devanagari: चैद्यदेहोत्थितं ज्योतिः वासुदेवमुपाविशत् । पश्यतां सर्वभूतानां उल्केव भुवि खाच्च्युता ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: caidyadehotthitaṃ jyotiḥ vāsudevamupāviśat paśyatāṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ ulkeva bhuvi khāccyutā
translation: Thereafter, like a meteor falling on the earth from the sky, there emerged an effulgent light from the body of Śiśupāla and entered the person of Vasudeva while all beings stood as witness to it.

devanagari: जन्मत्रयानुगुणित वैरसंरब्धया धिया । ध्यायन् तन्मयतां यातो भावो हि भवकारणम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: janmatrayānuguṇita vairasaṃrabdhayā dhiyā dhyāyan tanmayatāṃ yāto bhāvo hi bhavakāraṇam
translation: Pondering over the Lord with thoughts saturated with hatred and anger entertained for three consecutive lives, he (Śiśupāla) attained identity with the Lord, for it is one’s own brooding which determines one’s future state of life.

devanagari: ऋत्विग्भ्यः ससदस्येभ्यो दक्षिनां विपुलामदात् । सर्वान् संपूज्य विधिवत् चक्रेऽवभृथमेकराट् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛtvigbhyaḥ sasadasyebhyo dakṣināṃ vipulāmadāt sarvān saṃpūjya vidhivat cakre

translation: The emperor Yudhiṣṭhira gave liberal sacrificial fees to the priests who conducted the sacrifice and to the members of the sacrificial assembly. And having thus worshipped them all with due respect, he performed the closing ablution of the sacrifice (avabhṛtha snāna) according to the injunctions laid down in the śāstras.

devanagari: साधयित्वा क्रतुः राज्ञः कृष्णो योगेश्वरेश्वरः । उवास कतिचिन् मासान् सुहृद्भिः अभियाचितः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhayitvā kratuḥ rājñaḥ kṛṣṇo yogeśvareśvaraḥ uvāsa katicin māsān suhṛdbhiḥ abhiyācitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: After having accomplished the Rājasūya sacrifice successfully for king Yudhiṣṭhira, Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the masters of Yoga, lived some more months at Indraprastha at the request of his friends.

devanagari: ततोऽनुज्ञाप्य राजानं अनिच्छन्तमपीश्वरः । ययौ सभार्यः सामात्यः स्वपुरं देवकीसुतः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tato yayau sabhāryaḥ sāmātyaḥ svapuraṃ devakīsutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Thereafter, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, took leave of king Yudhiṣṭhira who was unwilling to let him go and returned to his capital along with his queens and counsellors.

devanagari: वर्णितं तदुपाख्यानं मया ते बहुविस्तरम् । वैकुण्ठवासिनोर्जन्म विप्रशापात् पुनः पुनः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: varṇitaṃ tadupākhyānaṃ mayā te bahuvistaram vaikuṇṭhavāsinorjanma vipraśāpāt punaḥ punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ varṇ class 10 verb]

translation: The long narrative of how (Jaya and Vijaya) the two residents of Vaikuṇṭha (as the attendants of Lord Viṣṇu) had to undergo repeated births on the earth under the curse of Brāhmaṇas (like Sanaka, etc.), has been described to you in details.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
vide supra Skandha VII. Ch.s 1-9.

devanagari: राजसूयावभृथ्येन स्नातो राजा युधिष्ठिरः । ब्रह्मक्षत्रसभामध्ये शुशुभे सुरराडिव ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: rājasūyāvabhṛthyena snāto rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ brahmakṣatrasabhāmadhye śuśubhe surarāḍiva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ snā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ snā class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ snā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ snā class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ snā class 4 verb]
[ablative single from √ snā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ snā class 2 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: When king Yudhiṣṭhira performed his closing ablution of the Ṛājasūya sacrifice he shone like Indra, the king of gods, in the assembly of Brāhmaṇas and Kṣattriyas.

devanagari: राज्ञा सभाजिताः सर्वे सुरमानवखेचराः । कृष्णं क्रतुं च शंसन्तः स्वधामानि ययुर्मुदा ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: rājñā sabhājitāḥ sarve suramānavakhecarāḥ kṛṣṇaṃ kratuṃ ca śaṃsantaḥ svadhāmāni yayurmudā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb]

translation: All the gods, men and celestial beings (who attended the sacrifice) were duly honoured by the king. They went back to their respective place praising with joy Lord Kṛṣṇa and the Ṛājasūya sacrifice.

devanagari: दुर्योधनमृते पापं कलिं कुरुकुलामयम् । यो न सेहे श्रीयं स्फीतां दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुसुतस्य ताम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: duryodhanamṛte pāpaṃ kaliṃ kurukulāmayam yo na sehe śrīyaṃ sphītāṃ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍusutasya tām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The sinful Duryodhana, the veritable Kali himself, who was a bane to the family of Kurus was the only exception. He could not bear the sight of the vast affluence and the royal splendour of Pāṇḍu’s son (Yudhiṣṭhira).

devanagari: य इदं कीर्तयेद् विष्णोः कर्म चैद्य वधादिकम् । राजमोक्षं वितानं च सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: ya idaṃ kīrtayed viṣṇoḥ karma caidya vadhādikam rājamokṣaṃ vitānaṃ ca sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate
translation:
He who sings of Lord Viṣṇu’s glorious deed of slaying Śiśupāla (and Jarāsandha and others) and the release of the kings taken captive (by Jarāsandha) and the performance of the Rājasūya sacrifice is released from the bondage of all sins.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । शिशुपालवधो नाम चतुःसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe śiśupālavadho nāma catuḥsaptatitamo
